patches.acceptance: The prepared Code - OSS tree uses Qivryn product identity, theme, data directories, URL protocol, and an in-box Qivryn extension.
Agents, editor, terminal, and review surfaces restore as one native workspace layout.
The Qivryn desktop IDE registers qivryn-agent as an in-box native Chat Session provider while the React surface remains a fallback for extension and JetBrains modes.
New built-in Chat sessions prefer qivryn-agent when the Qivryn provider contribution is available, without requiring a bundled Copilot default agent.
Deferred until native Agent Sessions are rebuilt against captured CodieApp references.
The current handoff is handled by the Qivryn Agents webview panel; native session handoff is deferred with native Agent Sessions.
Built-in and saved layouts capture supported workbench state, persist per workspace, and restore reliably.
Editor tabs display repository, branch, worktree, and run identity without extension-only decorations.
Products without a bundled default chat provider start without upstream Copilot onboarding errors.
The account service remains available and non-blocking when a product has no bundled default chat provider.
Saved layouts can read workbench visibility state through a Qivryn-internal, read-only command.
Agent worktrees register as native Git repositories so their branches and checkpoint commits appear as first-class source-control graph nodes.
Browser sessions render in a native editor webview with navigation, inspection, screenshots, recording, permissions, and agent takeover.
